steps.title: Connect crops to settlements
steps.content: The team's conclusion is based on diet: cattle ate cultivated millet, while sheep and goats ate mostly wild plants. Since cultivated crops would be found near human farming activity, evidence that cattle ate another human-cultivated crop would strengthen the link to human settlements.

steps.title: Strengthen the cattle evidence
steps.content: B blurs the difference by giving sheep and goats some millet, C concerns nutritional needs rather than location, and D suggests all three animals varied with settlement crops. Additional cultivated wheat in cattle diets reinforces the team's conclusion because it gives cattle a second diet marker associated with nearby humans. The answer is A.
